proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0.
Female patients (sex_female=1) have a different objective_response rate than male patients.
Patients with secondary_aml=1 have a different objective_response rate than de novo AML.
FLT3-ITD mutated patients have a different objective_response rate than FLT3-ITD wild-type.
IDH1-mutated patients have a different objective_response rate than IDH1 wild-type.
IDH2-mutated patients have a different objective_response rate than IDH2 wild-type.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ response 23.5% (n=15014) vs NPM1- 16.7% (n=34986); diff +6.7 pp; chi-square p=5.79e-70.
Complex karyotype response 16.8% (n=7462) vs non-complex 19.1% (n=42538); diff -2.3 pp; p=3.31e-06.
Response by ECOG-PS: 0=23.1% (n=17592), 1=17.4% (n=24971), 2=12.9% (n=7437); strong monotonic decrease (Cochran-Armitage trend p<<1e-50).
Unfit response 22.1% (n=20056) vs fit 16.5% (n=29944); diff +5.6 pp; p=1.88e-55. Counterintuitive direction in raw data; suspected confounding via ven/aza.
Female response 18.9% vs male 18.6%; diff +0.3 pp; p=0.42; no signal.
Secondary AML response 18.8% vs de novo 18.7%; p=0.85; no signal.
FLT3-ITD+ response 18.9% vs ITD- 18.7%; p=0.64; no overall signal.
IDH1+ response 19.1% vs IDH1- 18.7%; p=0.60; no overall signal.
IDH2+ response 19.1% vs IDH2- 18.7%; p=0.47; no overall signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher age_years is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Mean blast_pct_marrow 58.5 (responders) vs 60.3 (non-responders); r=-0.037, p=2.25e-16.
Mean albumin 3.82 (responders) vs 3.80 (non-responders); r=+0.023, p=2.83e-07.
Mean weight_loss_pct_6mo 3.44 (responders) vs 3.95 (non-responders); r=-0.052, p=8.01e-31.
Mean WBC 19.27 (responders) vs 20.39 (non-responders); r=-0.017, p=1.82e-04.
Mean CRP 5.69 (responders) vs 6.17 (non-responders); r=-0.020, p=6.05e-06.
Mean age 67.98 (responders) vs 68.00 (non-responders); r=-0.001, p=0.88; no signal in raw data.
Mean LDH 223.1 (responders) vs 224.0 (non-responders); r=-0.004, p=0.35; no signal.
Mean hemoglobin 12.48 (responders) vs 12.51 (non-responders); r=-0.006, p=0.15; no signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher objective_response rate than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Ven/Aza response 22.0% (n=20020) vs no Ven/Aza 16.6% (n=29980); diff +5.4 pp; p=6.62e-51. Strong overall positive effect.
7+3 response 18.9% vs no 7+3 18.6%; diff +0.3 pp; p=0.47; no overall effect.
Midostaurin response 18.6% vs no 18.8%; diff -0.2 pp; p=0.67; no overall effect.
Gilteritinib response 18.1% vs no 18.9%; diff -0.7 pp; p=0.14; no overall effect.
Ivosidenib response 18.8% vs no 18.7%; diff +0.1 pp; p=0.93; no overall effect.
Enasidenib response 18.6% vs no 18.8%; diff -0.2 pp; p=0.79; no overall effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among FLT3-ITD+ patients, treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response relative to no midostaurin (canonical biology).
Among FLT3-TKD+ patients, treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response relative to no midostaurin.
Among FLT3-ITD+ patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response relative to no gilteritinib.
Among FLT3-TKD+ patients, treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response relative to no gilteritinib.

analyses.result_summary: FLT3-ITD+: midostaurin on=17.6% (n=1468) vs off=19.1% (n=8532); diff -1.5 pp. Canonical FLT3+ benefit not observed.
FLT3-TKD+: midostaurin on=17.6% (n=443) vs off=18.1% (n=2372); diff -0.5 pp; not significant.
FLT3-ITD+: gilteritinib on=19.1% (n=1491) vs off=18.9% (n=8509); diff +0.2 pp; not significant.
FLT3-TKD+: gilteritinib on=15.1% (n=418) vs off=18.6% (n=2397); diff -3.5 pp; numerically negative, p=0.07.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among IDH1-mutated patients, treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response relative to no ivosidenib.
Among IDH2-mutated patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response relative to no enasidenib.

analyses.result_summary: IDH1+: ivosidenib on=15.4% (n=298) vs off=19.4% (n=3326); diff -4.0 pp. Canonical IDH1+ benefit not observed; numerically reversed.
IDH2+: enasidenib on=19.5% (n=354) vs off=19.1% (n=4325); diff +0.4 pp; not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is concentrated in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients and absent in unfit_for_intensive=0 patients (treatment-by-fitness interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Ven/Aza effect on response: in fit (unfit=0) on=16.0% vs off=16.8% (diff -0.8 pp, n_on=12007 n_off=17937); in unfit (unfit=1) on=30.8% vs off=16.3% (diff +14.5 pp, n_on=8013 n_off=12043). Massive heterogeneity by fitness.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect is concentrated in npm1_mutation=1 patients vs npm1_mutation=0 patients.
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect is suppressed by tp53_mutation=1.
The treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect is suppressed by complex_karyotype=1.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1-: Ven/Aza on=16.5% vs off=16.9% (diff -0.4 pp, n_on=13995 n_off=20991). NPM1+: Ven/Aza on=34.7% vs off=16.0% (diff +18.7 pp, n_on=6025 n_off=8989). Strong heterogeneity by NPM1 status.
TP53wt: Ven/Aza on=22.6% vs off=16.6% (+6.0 pp). TP53+: Ven/Aza on=16.4% vs off=16.7% (-0.4 pp). Effect lost when TP53 mutated.
Non-complex: Ven/Aza on=22.7% vs off=16.7% (+6.1 pp). Complex: Ven/Aza on=17.5% vs off=16.3% (+1.2 pp). Effect attenuated to ~null with complex karyotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all features in a multivariable logistic regression, the strongest independent positive predictors of objective_response are npm1_mutation, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine, unfit_for_intensive, and albumin_g_dl; strongest negative are ecog_ps, complex_karyotype, tp53_mutation, blast_pct_marrow, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, wbc_k_per_ul.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coefficients (significant): npm1_mutation +0.44 (p<1e-50), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine +0.36 (p<1e-50), unfit_for_intensive +0.37 (p<1e-50), albumin +0.12 (p=2e-7), ecog_ps -0.36 (p<1e-50), complex_karyotype -0.17 (p=6e-7), tp53_mutation -0.19 (p=4e-6), blast_pct_marrow -0.005 per pp (p<1e-15), weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.037 per pp (p<1e-30), crp_mg_l -0.0065 per mg/L (p=6e-6), wbc -0.0018 per k/uL (p=1e-4). 7+3, midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib all non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an adjusted logistic regression that adds one treatment-by-feature interaction at a time, only treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine shows large, significant interactions with unfit_for_intensive (positive), npm1_mutation (positive), tp53_mutation (negative), and complex_karyotype (negative); other treatment interactions are absent or marginal.

analyses.code: for tx in treatments: for f in modifiers: fit logit with tx*f interaction and full feature panel
analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted interactions (coef, p): ven/aza x unfit +0.90 (p=4e-80); ven/aza x npm1 +1.08 (p=4e-105); ven/aza x tp53 -0.45 (p=4e-8); ven/aza x complex -0.32 (p=2e-6); 7+3 x complex -0.23 (p=8e-4); enasidenib x sex_female +0.32 (p=2e-4); enasidenib x unfit +0.18 (p=0.04); gilteritinib x unfit +0.14 (p=0.04); ivosidenib x npm1 -0.19 (p=0.04). Ven/aza dominates the heterogeneity signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a much larger increase in objective_response than in any other unfit/npm1 cell.

analyses.result_summary: Ven/Aza diff by unfit x npm1 cell: (unfit=0, npm1=0) -1.0 pp; (unfit=0, npm1=1) -0.3 pp; (unfit=1, npm1=0) +0.5 pp; (unfit=1, npm1=1) +47.4 pp (on=63.3% n=2401 vs off=16.0% n=3585). Effect concentrated entirely in unfit AND npm1+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 patients, the ven/aza response benefit is abolished if tp53_mutation=1.

analyses.result_summary: Within unfit+npm1+: TP53wt ven/aza on=69.1% (n=2125) vs off=15.7% (n=3224), diff +53.4 pp. TP53+ ven/aza on=18.8% (n=276) vs off=18.3% (n=361), diff +0.6 pp. TP53 mutation completely abolishes the ven/aza effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 patients, the ven/aza response benefit is abolished if complex_karyotype=1.

analyses.result_summary: Within unfit+npm1+: non-complex ven/aza on=71.7% (n=2025) vs off=16.1% (n=3052), diff +55.6 pp. Complex ven/aza on=18.4% (n=376) vs off=15.2% (n=533), diff +3.2 pp. Complex karyotype completely abolishes the ven/aza effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The ven/aza response benefit is concentrated in the joint subgroup unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this exact subgroup the ven/aza effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Responder subgroup unfit=1 & npm1=1 & tp53=0 & complex=0 (n=4531, 9.1% of cohort): ven/aza on=78.6% (n=1790) vs off=15.8% (n=2741); diff +62.8 pp; chi-square p<<1e-300; OR=19.5. Outside this subgroup (n=45469): ven/aza on=16.4% vs off=16.7%; diff -0.3 pp; p=0.42 (null).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The unfit_for_intensive=1 predicate is necessary in the responder subgroup: among fit (unfit=0) patients with npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, ven/aza has no effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Fit + npm1+ + tp53wt + non-complex (n=6951): ven/aza on=15.8% (n=2789) vs off=16.1% (n=4162); diff -0.3 pp; not significant. Confirms unfit_for_intensive=1 is required for ven/aza efficacy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within unfit+npm1+tp53wt+non-complex, the ven/aza effect is robust across age, sex, ECOG, FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, and secondary AML status (no further effect modifiers); other treatments do not have a comparable effect inside this subgroup.

analyses.code: for f in [flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2, sex, secondary, age_bin, ecog]: stratify within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup ven/aza diff (on - off): FLT3-ITD-=+47.5pp, FLT3-ITD+=+46.4; FLT3-TKD-=+47.3, FLT3-TKD+=+48.1; IDH1-=+47.5, IDH1+=+45.3; IDH2-=+48.2, IDH2+=+40.5; sex_male=+47.0, sex_female=+47.8; secondary_AML- =+47.2, secondary+=+47.7; ECOG=0 +66.5, ECOG=1 +61.9, ECOG=2 +57.0 (effect persists at all levels); age 0-60 +65.5, 60-70 +63.1, 70-80 +62.2, 80-100 +58.3 (modest age attenuation but uniformly large). None of these features is a meaningful additional modifier. Within subgroup midostaurin (p=1.0), gilteritinib (p=1.0), ivosidenib (p=0.5), 7+3 (p=0.8) all null; enasidenib weakly +5.9 pp (p=0.03, likely chance).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, after adjusting for age, sex, ECOG, FLT3-ITD, FLT3-TKD, IDH1, IDH2, secondary AML, WBC, blast_pct_marrow, albumin, LDH, and the other treatment indicators, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains a strong independent positive predictor of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit within responder subgroup: ven/aza coef +3.05, OR=21.0, p<<1e-300. Effect dominates all other adjustments. Univariate within-subgroup OR ~20.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among complex_karyotype=1 patients, treatment_7plus3 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than no 7+3 (treatment is less effective in chemorefractory karyotypes).

analyses.result_summary: Non-complex: 7+3 on=19.5% vs off=18.8% (diff +0.7 pp). Complex: 7+3 on=15.5% (n=3415) vs off=17.9% (n=4047) (diff -2.3 pp). Adjusted interaction coef -0.23 (p=8.3e-4). Modest negative interaction; 7+3 numerically harms response in complex karyotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enasidenib has a more positive effect on objective_response in female (sex_female=1) than male patients (sex_female=0).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction enasidenib x sex_female coef +0.32 (p=2.5e-4). Raw: men on=16.5% (n=2191) vs off=18.8% (diff -2.3); women on=21.2% (n=1781) vs off=18.7% (diff +2.5). Statistically significant but small in absolute terms; biologic mechanism unclear so likely incidental given many tests, but worth noting.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and 7+3, no clean two-feature subgroup shows a treatment effect comparable in magnitude or biological plausibility to the ven/aza responder subgroup.

analyses.code: exhaustive 2-way subgroup screen across all binary feature pairs and all 5 non-venaza treatments, requiring n_on>=80, n_off>=80, |diff|>=0.05
analyses.result_summary: Largest 2-way subgroup signals: gilteritinib in flt3_tkd+&secondary_aml+ -8.5 pp (n=96); 7+3 in flt3_tkd+&idh2+ +9.9 pp (n=118); ivosidenib in flt3_tkd+&sex_female=1 +8.9 pp (n=88) but in flt3_tkd+&sex_male -6.1 pp; gilteritinib in flt3_itd+&idh1+ +6.5 pp (n=118). All effects modest (<10 pp), inconsistent across cells, with small n, and no biologically coherent pattern. None survives multiple-testing correction or replicates across related features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_gilteritinib is more effective in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients than in fit patients.
Treatment_enasidenib is more effective in unfit_for_intensive=1 AND idh2_mutation=1 patients than in other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction gilteritinib x unfit coef +0.14 (p=0.04). Raw: fit on=15.4% vs off=16.6% (diff -1.2 pp); unfit on=22.5% vs off=22.0% (diff +0.5 pp). Marginal; the apparent positive interaction reflects reduced harm in unfit vs slightly negative effect in fit, not a true responder subgroup.
IDH2+ unfit: enasidenib on=29.1% (n=141) vs off=23.1% (n=1748); diff +6.0 pp (p~0.07). Suggestive but not strongly significant; small n. Best candidate enasidenib responder subgroup (still modest).

proposed_hypotheses.text: When restricted to patients OUTSIDE the unfit+npm1+tp53wt+non-complex subgroup, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has no detectable effect on objective_response (effect estimate near zero, not significant).

analyses.result_summary: Outside responder subgroup (n=45469, 90.9%): ven/aza on=16.4% (n=18230) vs off=16.7% (n=27239); diff -0.3 pp; p=0.42; chi-square non-significant. Effect is fully concentrated in the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Removing any single predicate from {unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0} eliminates the ven/aza response benefit, demonstrating all four predicates are necessary.

analyses.code: for each predicate-relaxation test diff in the leave-one-out cell that should not benefit
analyses.result_summary: Drop unfit (i.e., fit+npm1+tp53wt+non-complex): diff -0.3 pp (null). Drop npm1 (unfit+npm1-+tp53wt+non-complex): diff +0.5 pp (null). Add tp53 mutation (unfit+npm1+tp53+ +non-complex): diff +0.6 pp (null). Add complex karyotype (unfit+npm1+tp53wt+ complex): diff +3.2 pp (small, near null). All four predicates are jointly required; each predicate failure independently reduces the ven/aza effect to near zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the four targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib), there is no biologically expected mutation-targeted response subgroup in this dataset; their effects in target-mutation subpopulations are null or numerically reversed.

analyses.result_summary: Canonical target-subgroup effects: midostaurin in FLT3-ITD+ -1.5 pp (p=0.16); midostaurin in FLT3-TKD+ -0.5 pp (p=0.79); gilteritinib in FLT3-ITD+ +0.2 pp (p=0.84); gilteritinib in FLT3-TKD+ -3.5 pp (p=0.07, opposite direction); ivosidenib in IDH1+ -4.0 pp (p=0.07, opposite direction); enasidenib in IDH2+ +0.4 pp (p=0.84). None reach the canonical positive responder pattern that would be expected from regulatory trial data.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: The objective_response benefit of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is concentrated in the joint subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0, where ven/aza increases response from ~16% to ~79% (raw diff +63 pp; adjusted OR ~21). Outside this exact subgroup, the ven/aza effect on objective_response is null.

analyses.code: Final summary; adjusted logit within and outside subgroup; necessity tests
analyses.result_summary: Final responder subgroup definition: unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0 (n=4531; 9.1% of cohort). Within: ven/aza response 78.6% vs 15.8% off (diff +62.8 pp; adjusted OR=21.0; p<<1e-300). Outside: ven/aza diff -0.3 pp (p=0.42). Necessity confirmed: dropping any of the four predicates leaves the ven/aza diff at <=3 pp. This is the strongest treatment-effect heterogeneity finding in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: For the other treatments (treatment_7plus3, treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib) no responder subgroup with a treatment effect comparable in magnitude or robustness to the ven/aza subgroup is identifiable. The strongest secondary signals are (a) treatment_7plus3 is less effective in complex_karyotype=1 patients (interaction coef -0.23, p=8e-4) and (b) treatment_enasidenib in IDH2-mutated unfit_for_intensive=1 patients shows a small +6 pp response increase (p~0.07), neither of which approaches the ven/aza signal.

analyses.code: Synthesized from prior systematic interaction screening and 2-way subgroup screens
analyses.result_summary: No other treatment shows a clean responder subgroup. Best candidates: 7+3 x complex_karyotype interaction (negative, modest); enasidenib in unfit IDH2+ (suggestive +6 pp). Both are far below ven/aza-in-responder-subgroup magnitude. Targeted therapies do not show their canonical mutation-matched benefits in this dataset.
